title: Unified design-token / theming system — how Web Everything structures the concrete-value layer
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #364 (the design-token project #010 deferred as "the technical half"). The W3C Design Tokens Community Group reached its first stable format (DTCG 2025.10, Oct 2025); the native CSS substrate (custom properties, @property typed registration, light-dark()/color-scheme, relative color syntax + color-mix) is Baseline; and the benchmark design systems (MD3 ref/sys/comp, Fluent global/alias, Spectrum, Radix 12-step scales, Apple HIG dynamic system colors) are unanimous on a 2-3 tier primitive→semantic→component taxonomy. The WE-specific reframe: WE already owns the semantic tier as Intents (surface/density/typography/motion + theme-color #010), so the token project should own the primitive + component tiers and leave the semantic tier to the intents — not coin a parallel vocabulary. Grounds four forks: home/layer, format (adopt DTCG), tier taxonomy + the #010 seam, and scheme/accent-derivation (native-auto vs curated — the one divergent call). Report: reports/2026-06-12-design-token-theming-system.md.
